title: Find the Top Talker in a Log
description:
## Who Hit Us the Most?

A file **`/root/ips.log`** contains one client IP per line (with repeats).

**Your task:** determine the **single most frequent IP** and write it (just the address) to **`/root/top.txt`**.

> 💡 The classic pipeline is `sort | uniq -c | sort -nr` to rank by count, then take the first line and strip the count. `uniq -c` needs its input sorted first.

hints.title: Count, rank, pick
hints.body: Sort so identical lines are adjacent, count with uniq -c, sort numerically descending, take the top line, then print just the IP field.

validation.description: Checks that /root/top.txt holds the most frequent IP (1.1.1.1).
